zh.lifeSummary: García Gómez Carrillo de Molina是Garcia Gomez Carillo和Urraca Alfonso Molina de León的兒子，出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多。他於1284年之前與Elvira Álvarez Osorio在西班牙劍橋大學默里 卡斯利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1312年在西班牙迪奧希斯 德 托萊多。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: García Gómez Carrillo de Molina是Garcia Gomez Carillo和Urraca Alfonso Molina de León的儿子，出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多。他于1284年之前与Elvira Álvarez Osorio在西班牙劍橋大學默里 卡斯利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1312年在西班牙迪奧希斯 德 托萊多。

nameMeanings.name: Gómez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Francisco, Miguel, Pedro, Jorge, Ramon, Rafael.
Spanish (Gómez): from a medieval personal name, probably of Visigothic origin, from guma ‘man’. Compare De Gomez .

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the name in 1 above at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some Jewish descendants retained the surname that their families had adopted as Roman Catholics.

variants.name: De Gomez
meanings: Spanish (De Gómez): patronymic from Gómez (see Gomez ), with the preposition de ‘of’.

variants.name: Gomes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Carlos, Fernando, Luis, Francisco, Jorge, Mario, Domingos, Jaime, Julio, Luiz.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Paulo, Catarina, Henrique, Agostinho, Amadeu, Ademir, Aderito, Aloisio, Conceicao, Ilidio.

Portuguese and Galician: from the medieval personal name Gomes, probably Visigothic in origin, from guma ‘man’. This surname is also found on the west coast of India, where it was taken by Portuguese colonists.
Spanish: variant of Gómez (see Gomez ).

nameMeanings.name: Carrillo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Carlos, Jesus, Manuel, Luis, Pedro, Francisco, Ruben, Miguel, Roberto, Mario.

Spanish: nickname for a person with some peculiarity of the cheek or jaw, Spanish carrillo. The word appears to be a diminutive of carro ‘cart, wagon’, and it has been suggested that the reference is to the movements of the jaw in chewing. The surname may also have denoted originally a bold or shameless person; for the semantic development compare Cheek .
Italian (Campania): probably from a diminutive of carro (see Carro ), unless it derives from the Spanish (see 1 above).

variants.name: Cheek
meanings: English: from Middle English cheeke (Old English cē(a)ce), used as a nickname for someone with some deformity or scar in the region of the cheek or jawbone. Compare Cheak and Chick .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Molina
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Manuel, Jesus, Francisco, Jorge, Mario, Miguel, Ana, Roberto.  Portuguese Joao, Margarida.

Spanish, Catalan, and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name from any of numerous places called Molina, in particular the one in Guadalajara province. This surname is also found in France (compare   Moline  ). For certain Jewish families, this is an adoption of the Iberian surname at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Moline
meanings: Americanized form of Swedish   Molin  .
Catalan (Moliné): occupational name for a miller, from moliner miller. This surname is also found in southern France.
French and Jewish (from France): altered form of   Molina  , a surname of Spanish and Catalan origin.

nameMeanings.name: García
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Often used as a feminine equivalent of Mark , but in fact a feminine form of Marcius, itself a derivative of Marcus . One St Marcia is commemorated in a group with Felix, Luciolus, Fortunatus, and others; another with Zenais, Cyria, and Valeria; and a third with Ariston, Crescentian, Eutychian, Urban, Vitalis, Justus, Felicissimus, Felix, and Symphorosa. None is individually very famous.

variants.name: Marsha
meanings: Phonetic spelling of   Marcia  , associated particularly with the American film star  Marsha Hunt  (  b. 1917 ).

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: García Gómez Carrillo de Molina was born in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain as the son of Garcia Gomez Carillo and Urraca Alfonso Molina de León. He married Elvira Álvarez Osorio before 1284, in New Castile, Spain. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died in 1312, in Diócesis de Toledo, Spain.

ko.lifeSummary: García Gómez Carrillo de Molina(은)는 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 Garcia Gomez Carillo(와)과 Urraca Alfonso Molina de León의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1284년 이전 스페인 뉴 카스틸에서 Elvira Álvarez Osorio(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1312년 스페인 디o스시스 데 톨도에서 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Elvira Álvarez Osorio antes 1284, em New Castile, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1312, em Diócesis de Toledo, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: García Gómez Carrillo de Molina родился в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания. Его родителей звали Garcia Gomez Carillo и Urraca Alfonso Molina de León. Он женился на женщине по имени Elvira Álvarez Osorio перед 1284 г. в местности ню кастиль, Испания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1312 г. в местности диoсесис де толедо, Испания.

Se casó con Elvira Álvarez Osorio antes de 1284, en New Castile, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió en 1312, en Diócesis de Toledo, España.
